metadata_json.memo_preview_snippet: Found packet values, formulas for elapsed minutes and decay, QC classification, repeat logic and sensitivity checks; final label WARN and disposition RETEST_THEN_RELEASE_IF_PASS.

rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Minor documentation/formula-mapping discrepancy: uplift applied multiplicatively instead of explicit additive +1.2 percentage points; workbook not parseable in preview so cell-level verification is inferred.
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Open the workbook with a parser and confirm cell addresses and that the uplift is implemented as an additive +1.2 percentage points (e.g., new_RCP_pct = old_RCP_pct + 1.2) per the intake spec. In the workbook, use explicit named cells (e.g., repeat_uplift_pct_points = 1.2) and a clearly documented formula (e.g., =B10 + repeat_uplift_pct_points) to avoid ambiguity. Attach a short table in the memo showing the numeric activity-at-times calculations (with values) copied from the workbook to allow byte-level review when the workbook cannot be parsed automatically.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present. The memo contains explicit row-level citations to the embedded packet values (calibration_time, requested_injection_time, initial_activity_GBq, half_life_min, min_activity_GBq_at_dispatch, QC measurements). The decay formula shown (A(t)=A0*2^(-t/half_life)) and the elapsed-time formula are consistent with the required half-life computation and time-difference logic. The memo documents repeat logic, sensitivity checks, and a final justified decision (WARN / RETEST_THEN_RELEASE_IF_PASS). Calculated projected activity after repeat remains above the min_activity_GBq_at_dispatch, and the projected RCP after uplift exceeds spec. However there is a minor implementation/documentation discrepancy in how the +1.2 uplift is applied in the workbook memo: the projection uses a multiplicative form (B10*(1+B8) producing 95.2292%) rather than an explicit additive '+1.2 percentage points' (which would yield 95.3%); this is a minor documentation/formula mapping issue but does not change the release gating outcome in this case. Also the actual workbook could not be programmatically opened for cell-level verification (openpyxl unavailable) so workbook-level formula correctness is inferred from the memo and trace rather than byte-level inspection.

decision.label: WARN
decision.recommended_action: RETEST once; release only if repeat RCP >=95% and all other QC pass

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include explicit workbook cell references or a short exported table of key formula cells in the memo (e.g., cell addresses and formula text for the decay and repeat calculations) to make verification of executable formulas easier. Also include an explicit line-by-line citation mapping to the embedded packet row IDs to satisfy strict row-level citation expectations.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present. Memo lists embedded-packet inputs (calibration_time 08:00, requested_injection_time 11:10, initial_activity_GBq 31.4, half_life_min 109.8, min_activity_GBq_at_dispatch 4.8, measured RCP 94.1%). Time delta correctly computed as 190 min (08:00->11:10). Decay formula used matches half-life form (A = A0 * 0.5^(t/half_life)); computed injection activity 9.46 GBq and post-retest (190+18 min) 8.45 GBq, both > min_activity 4.8 so retest permitted. RCP initial fail (94.1% < 95%), projected uplift to 95.3% consistent with applying historical uplift and required uplift computed as 0.9 points. Memo documents sensitivity (required uplift 0.9, projected margin 0.3) and explicitly limits recommendation to a single retest and release only if wet retest >=95.0 and other QC remain passing. Trace shows workbook produced with embedded formulas (formula_count metadata) and a corrected decision-cell reference, supporting executable-formula requirement. No invented external numeric values detected; all numbers are either packet inputs or computed from them.
